%% =====================================================================
%% Graphical abstract for NMR in Biomedicine (uploaded as a SEPARATE item).
%% WileyNJD-v2 has no graphical-abstract macro, so the GA is its own document.
%% Image: figures/graphical_abstract.pdf (50 x 60 mm), regenerated by
%%        make_graphical_abstract.py from Sextant/results/railing_results.json.
%% NOTE: output basename deliberately differs from the included image
%%       (figures/graphical_abstract.pdf) so tectonic will write the output.
%% Build: tectonic graphical_abstract_card.tex  ->  graphical_abstract_card.pdf
%% =====================================================================
\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}
\usepackage[paperwidth=190mm,paperheight=85mm,margin=6mm]{geometry}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\graphicspath{{figures/}}
\input{numbers.tex}
\newcommand{\Ds}{D^{*}}
\pagestyle{empty}

\begin{document}
\noindent
\begin{minipage}[c]{52mm}
\includegraphics[width=50mm]{graphical_abstract.pdf}
\end{minipage}\hfill
\begin{minipage}[c]{118mm}
\small
A conventional bounded least-squares IVIM fit rails the pseudo-diffusion
coefficient $\Ds$ against a parameter bound in \HomoFrac\% of high-SNR voxels of an
open in-vivo abdomen scan (95\% CI [\HomoCIlo, \HomoCIhi]) --- an assumption-free,
reproducible signature of weak $\Ds$ identifiability that recurs at
\LihcFourFrac--\LihcThreeFrac\% across independent abdomen and liver cohorts.
Because the fraction is read directly off the optimiser, it needs no ground truth
and is immune to the objection that a calibration claim inherits the trust of its
simulator.
\end{minipage}
\end{document}
